rag_rules: Assumptions and guarantees for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use visible feature names and named constants in AGREE contracts when specifying bounds or relations.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and expressions in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in annex agree only when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Use valid AADL scoping rules: component type contracts in type scope, implementation bindings in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Avoid mismatched types in AGREE helper declarations and expressions.
